# Kit vs Beehiiv: For Newsletter Operators in 2026

## Summary

Practitioner comparison framing the two platforms as fundamentally different product categories: Beehiiv is a "newsletter business platform" (media company model), Kit is a "creator ecosystem" (sell digital products). The choice depends on your business model, not just feature preferences.

## Key quotations / statistics

- "Use Beehiiv if you're building a newsletter as a business — it has built-in monetization (native ad network, paid subscriptions with zero revenue share on paid plans, referral programs, boosts)."
- "Use Kit (ConvertKit) if you're a creator who needs the most generous free tier (10K subscribers free) and focuses on selling digital products or courses."
- "Kit had a significant price increase in September 2025 - up to 4x for some users. Verify current pricing."
- "Beehiiv has a slight edge for newsletter-specific deliverability optimization."
- Both handle deliverability infrastructure. "The bigger factor is your list quality and engagement."
- Kit free tier: 10,000 subscribers (most generous in industry). Beehiiv free tier: 2,500 subscribers with unlimited emails.

## Ghost comparison

- "Ghost is self-hosted or Ghost Pro ($25/month). Zero revenue share, full customization, built-in memberships. Better for technical operators who want complete ownership."

## Decision matrix

| Choose Beehiiv | Choose Kit | Choose Ghost |
|---|---|---|
| Newsletter as business (media company) | Creator selling digital products | Complete ownership desired |
| Monetization priority (ad network, paid subs) | Most generous free tier (10K subs) | Technically comfortable |
| SEO-optimized web hosting for archive | Landing page + email + product sales | Website + newsletter + membership |
| Scale beyond 2,500 subscribers | Primary revenue from digital products, not newsletters | Zero platform fees |
| Zero revenue share on paid subs | Creator ecosystem (Creator Network) | |

## Annotations for stinger-forge

- This source provides the cleanest framing for `guides/00-platform-selection.md`: it's about business model alignment, not features.
- The "creator ecosystem vs newsletter business platform" dichotomy is the key opening frame for the platform selection guide.
- The Ghost positioning here (technical users, complete ownership) gives the third anchor point in a three-platform comparison.
- Deliverability equality note is important: don't oversell platform deliverability differences; list quality matters more.
